instruction: Walk through kitchen. Make left after spider plant. Wait at open door on left.

system_prompt:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"Walk through kitchen. Make left after spider plant. Wait at open door on left. "

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of 500 movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
first_prompt: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.

tool_schemas.name: observe
tool_schemas.description:
Look through the robot's forward-facing camera.

Returns the current egocentric RGB view plus a clearance readout: distance in meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view (10.0 = open, 10 m or more). If the goal object is centered ahead, clearance "center" is your true distance to it. Pure read — does not advance the simulator or consume step budget.

tool_schemas.name: look_around
tool_schemas.description:
Scan the surroundings in ONE call: rotates a full 360 degrees and
    returns four labeled views — ahead (0°), right (+90°), behind (+180°),
    left (+270°) — then restores the original heading exactly.

    Costs 24 low-level turn steps from the step budget but only one tool
    call. Use this at junctions and decision points instead of turning 15
    degrees at a time; your position and final heading are unchanged.

tool_schemas.name: step
tool_schemas.description:
Execute a sequence of movement actions, in order.

Actions: 0 = STOP (permanently ENDS the episode — issue it only when you believe the robot is within 3 meters of the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.

Executes sequentially and halts early if the episode ends. Returns how many actions ran, total steps taken, remaining budget, and whether the episode is over. The camera view changes after stepping — call observe() to see the result. Note: when plenty of budget remains, your FIRST STOP request is withheld pending a placement check — call step([0]) again to confirm and execute it.

text: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.
I'll start by observing my surroundings.
I see a kitchen counter area. Let me scan around to understand the layout.

The "left" view (+270°) shows a kitchen corridor extending forward — that's the direction to walk through the kitchen. There's a doorway on the right (+90°) leading to what looks like a bathroom. The left view shows the open kitchen hallway I should walk through.

Let me turn left to face the kitchen corridor.
I'm now facing the kitchen corridor. There's a counter with a sink on the right. Clearance center is only 1.4m though — I may need to angle slightly. Let me move forward through the kitchen.
Center clearance dropped to 0.4m — there's an obstacle ahead. Let me observe.
There's a wall/counter corner ahead on the right. The corridor continues to the left. Left clearance is 2.6m. Let me turn left slightly to follow the corridor.

Now I see the kitchen corridor clearly ahead. There's a stove on the left, and the corridor continues forward. The right side has a fridge (0.3m close). Let me move forward through the kitchen corridor.
Good, center clearance opened up to 2.7m. I'm moving through the kitchen. The corridor continues ahead into a brighter open area. Let me continue forward through the kitchen.
I can now see more of the kitchen opening into a larger area. I need to look for a spider plant to make a left turn after it. Let me continue forward and look for the spider plant.

The kitchen is opening up into a living/dining area. I see plants on the counter to the left. Right clearance is now 4.0m — opening up. Let me look at the plant on the left counter more closely. There appear to be plants around. Let me continue a bit and scan for the spider plant.

Now I have a fuller picture. The right view (+90°) shows a spacious living/dining area — this is where the kitchen opens up. The left view shows the stovetop counter. Ahead the corridor continues.

I need to find a spider plant, then turn left after it. Let me look at the plant on the left counter in the "ahead" view — there's greenery. Let me move forward a little more to identify the spider plant, keeping an eye on the left side.
I've reached the end of the kitchen counter area. There's a green plant visible ahead near the windows. The area opens up. Let me move forward toward the open area where I can better see plants.

I've exited the kitchen into an open living area. I see a large plant near the window ahead. There's a wall/pillar in the center. Let me look around to spot the spider plant and any open doors on the left.

Now I have a clearer picture. Behind me (+180°) is the kitchen I came through. Ahead is the open living area with plants near the windows. To the left (+270°) there's a pillar and a hallway with framed art. 

I see a plant near the window ahead - I need to identify the spider plant. The ahead view shows a plant on the right near the window. Let me move forward into the living area to get closer to the plant and identify it.

I'm now up against the pillar/wall (center 0.5m). The open area is to the right. I see a plant near the window ahead-right. Let me turn right to move around the pillar into the open living space where the plant is.

Now I can see the open living area more fully. There's a plant on the left counter (near the pillar) and there are plants near the windows. I see a plant on the left edge on top of the counter. Let me look at the plant on the left — that might be the spider plant. Let me observe the left side more carefully by turning left slightly.

There's a small white cabinet on the left with something on top (near left, 0.3m). Let me look at that plant more closely — there's a plant on the white cabinet to the left. The instruction says "make left after spider plant." 

Let me examine the plant on the left counter. I'll turn left to face it.

I'm now facing the pillar/wall very close (0.4m). There's a doorway/hallway to the far left. The plant is on the cabinet on the right. Let me back up and reassess. I turned too far into the wall. Let me turn right back toward the open area.

Let me reconsider my strategy. I'm near a pillar and there's a plant on the white cabinet. Let me move into the open living area (to the right), then look for the spider plant and the open door on the left. First let me move forward/right into the open space.

Now I'm in the open living area. I can see the room better. There's a plant on the far left near the window and one near the pillar on the left. Let me move forward into the living room to get a full view and locate the spider plant precisely.

Now I have a good overview. Ahead there's a plant near the window (there's a green plant to the left of the window area). The right view (+90°) shows a large open living room with sofas. The left (+270°) shows framed pictures on a wall. Behind (+180°) is the kitchen.

The plant near the window ahead looks like it could be the spider plant (the leafy green plant near the glass door on the left of the ahead view). Let me approach that plant. I'll move forward toward the window/plant area.

Now I can clearly see a plant with long spiky green leaves on the counter/table in front of the window — that's a spider plant (with its characteristic arching, spiky leaves). This is the spider plant referenced in the instruction!

According to the instruction, I should make a left after the spider plant. The spider plant is right in front of me. Let me now identify where "left" leads and look for the open door. Let me look around from here to spot the open door on the left.

I'm now very close to the spider plant on the counter. It's clearly the spider plant with the long arching green leaves. Now I need to make a left after it. Let me turn left to see what's there — there should be an open door on the left.

Now I've turned left and I'm facing a wall with framed pictures. This is a dead-end wall. The open door must be in a different direction. Let me look around to find the open door on the left after the spider plant.

Let me reconsider. The spider plant was ahead of me (to my right now, +90° view shows the spider plant). The instruction says walk through kitchen, make left after spider plant. 

Looking at behind view (+180°): this is the open living room area with a dark wood-floored section on the left — that could lead to a door. The left view (+270°) shows a hallway going back toward the kitchen with a doorway/room.

Let me think about the path: I came from the kitchen (which is now toward the left/behind). I reached the spider plant. "Make left after spider plant" — as I was walking toward the plant, left would be... Let me reconsider orientation. When I was facing the spider plant (looking at the window), left would turn me toward where the framed pictures are (the wall). But that's a dead end.

Actually the behind view shows a room extension with wood flooring leading somewhere. Let me explore. Let me turn to face the behind direction and look for an open door.

Now I'm facing the large open living room. There's a wood-floored area on the left with what looks like a glass wall/window and possibly a door on the far left. The living room extends to the right with sofas.

Let me reconsider the instruction geometry. I walked through the kitchen heading toward the living room. The spider plant is on the counter near the window. "Make left after spider plant" — after passing the plant, I should turn left. 

The open door on the left is what I'm looking for. On the far left there's a glass sliding door area (leads outside). Let me explore the left side of the room. Let me turn left to examine that wood-floored area with the glass wall.

Interesting! Now I can see the spider plant is right in front of me on the table (the one with arching leaves). And ahead there's an OPEN DOOR — a wooden framed glass door that's open, leading into another room (looks like a dining/hallway area). This looks like the "open door" from the instruction!

But wait — I need to "make left after spider plant" and "wait at open door on left." Let me reassess. The open door ahead of me leads to another room. Let me get closer and check the layout. The door on the left is the target.

Let me look around here to precisely identify the open door on the left.

Now this is clearer. Ahead (0°) I see the spider plant on the table and an open wooden door beyond it. The open door ahead leads into another room. This appears to be the "open door on left" area.

Let me reconsider the whole path. I came through the kitchen. The spider plant is on this table. "Make left after spider plant" then "wait at open door on left."

Looking at the ahead view: the spider plant is in the foreground, and there's an open door (glass/wood framed) leading to the next room slightly to the center-left. That open door is my target.

Let me move toward the open door ahead. It's about 2.4m in center. Let me approach it.

I'm now right at the table with the spider plant (center 0.7m - the table is in front of me). The open door is ahead and slightly right, leading into the next room with wood flooring. I need to go around the table. Let me turn right slightly to go around it toward the open door.

Now I've gone around the table. I can see the open door on the left (the wooden framed glass doors are open, leading to the wood-floored room). The open door is now to my left. This matches "wait at open door on left."

Let me move forward to approach the open door and position myself there.

The open door (wooden framed glass doors) is now clearly on my left. I can see the open doorway leading into the wood-floored room. The instruction says "wait at open door on left." Let me approach and get right up to that open door. Let me turn left toward it and move closer.

Hmm, I've turned back toward the spider plant table (I see the plant again on the left and the table close ahead at 0.7m). I overcorrected. The open door is the wooden-framed opening ahead/right. Let me reposition. Let me turn right to face the open door.

Now I can see the open door clearly on my left (the wooden-framed glass door opening leading to the wood-floored room). The center is open (2.3m). 

The instruction is "wait at open door on left." I'm now positioned with the open door on my left. The open doorway is the wooden framed opening. Let me move forward toward the doorway threshold to be right at it.

The open door is now on my left (the wooden-framed glass doors that are open, leading into the wood-floored room). I'm right beside it. The doorway threshold is to my left. 

The instruction says "wait at open door on left." I'm now positioned right at the open door with it on my left. This is the endpoint. Let me move just slightly to be right at the door threshold and confirm my position by turning to face the door.

Now I'm facing back toward the spider plant table area and the window. I see the spider plant on the right (0.7m). Let me turn to face the open door directly. The open door was to my left before I turned. Let me turn right to face the open wooden door.

Now I'm facing back toward the kitchen/living area (I can see the kitchen island). I've turned 180° from the door. The open door is now behind me. 

Let me reconsider my orientation carefully. I'm getting a bit disoriented. Let me do a full look_around to establish where the open door is precisely.

Now the layout is clear:
- **Left (+270°)**: The OPEN DOOR — the wooden-framed glass doors that are open, leading into the wood-floored room. This is the open door on the left!
- **Behind (+180°)**: The spider plant table near the window.
- **Right (+90°)**: The wall with framed pictures.
- **Ahead (0°)**: Back toward the kitchen island.

The open door is to my left (+270°). The instruction: "Make left after spider plant. Wait at open door on left." I passed the spider plant and the open door is on my left. 

I'm currently positioned near the open door. Let me face the open door (turn left) and approach the threshold to "wait at" it.

Now I'm facing the open door area. The open wooden-framed door is ahead-left, leading into the wood-floored room. The doorway threshold with the wood flooring is visible. Let me move forward to reach the open door threshold.

I'm approaching the open door. The wood-floored area (the threshold of the open door) is visible on the left. The open wooden-framed glass doors are on my left. Let me continue forward a bit and turn to be right at the open door on the left.

I'm now right in front of the open door on my left. The wooden-framed open glass doors lead into the wood-floored room (visible bottom-center with the wood plank floor). I am right at the open door — this is the endpoint: "wait at open door on left."

Let me turn to face the open door to confirm I'm at it, then position precisely.

Now I can see clearly: the spider plant is on the table in front of me (center), and the OPEN DOOR is on the right (the wooden-framed open glass doors leading into the wood-floored room). 

Wait — the open door appears on my right now, not left. Let me re-examine. The instruction was "make left after spider plant, wait at open door on left." 

I'm now facing the spider plant with the open door to the right. If I position so the spider plant is behind me and I approach the door, the door would be... Let me reconsider. The key is: I'm right at the open door. It's within a couple meters. The open door threshold (wood floor) is clearly visible and I'm about 1.5-2m from it.

I am at the open door, which is the goal. I'm within 3 meters of it. Let me position right at the door threshold. The door is to my right-front. Let me turn slightly right toward it.

I'm now right at the open door threshold. The wood-floored room is directly ahead-left through the open wooden-framed glass doors. I can see the threshold with the wood plank flooring right in front of me. 

I've completed the instruction:
1. ✅ Walked through the kitchen
2. ✅ Made a left after the spider plant
3. ✅ Reached the open door on the left

I'm now positioned right at the open door. Let me confirm I'm at the threshold and stop here. The open door is directly in front/left of me and I'm within 3 meters. Let me move one small step to be right at it and stop.

I'm now right at the open door. The open wooden-framed glass doors are directly in front of me (center 2.3m into the wood-floored room). I'm positioned at the threshold of the open door on the left as instructed. 

I've reached the endpoint: walked through the kitchen, made left after the spider plant, and I'm now at the open door on the left. I'm within 3 meters of the goal. Let me issue STOP.

texts: {"stop_withheld": true, "message": "STOP not executed (first request). You still have 23 tool calls \u2014 verify placement before committing: (1) look_around(); (2) re-read the instruction's FINAL clause word by word; (3) apply the placement rules (several similar spots -> the one farther along your travel direction; 'all the way / to the end' -> keep going until center clearance < 1.5 m; 'door/doorway' -> stand at the opening). Move if a better spot exists, then call step([0]) again to execute it.", "tool_calls_used": 77, "tool_calls_remaining": 23}

text: The STOP was withheld for verification. Let me re-read the final clause: "Wait at open door on left." I should stand at the opening of the door. Let me do a look_around to verify placement.

Now I can verify placement clearly:
- **Ahead (0°)**: The OPEN DOOR — wooden-framed glass doors, open, leading into the wood-floored room. I'm standing right at this opening (center 2.3m into the room).
- **Left (+270°)**: The spider plant on the counter/table near the window.
- **Behind (+180°)**: Wall with framed pictures.
- **Right (+90°)**: The living room/kitchen island area.

I'm positioned right at the open door opening. The spider plant is on my left, and I made the left turn after it to reach this open door. This matches the instruction perfectly: "Make left after spider plant. Wait at open door on left."

I'm standing at the door opening (the threshold with wood flooring is directly ahead). This is exactly where I should "wait." I'm well within 3 meters of the open door. My placement is correct. Let me confirm STOP.
